document_text:
LEGAL NOTICE

To: Mr. Rajesh Kumar, No. 45, 4th Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore - 560011

Subject: Notice under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881 regarding dishonour of cheque

Sir,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client Mr. Suresh Patel, I serve upon you the following notice:

1. That you had issued Cheque No. 002145 dated 12-03-2026 drawn on HDFC Bank, Jayanagar Branch, for a sum of Rs. 3,50,000/- (Rupees Three Lakh Fifty Thousand only) in favour of my client towards repayment of a loan advanced to you.

2. That the said cheque, when presented for encashment by my client through his banker on 15-03-2026, was returned unpaid vide Bank's memo dated 18-03-2026 with the remark "Funds Insufficient".

3. That my client received intimation of the said dishonour from his bank on 19-03-2026.

4. You are hereby called upon to pay the said sum of Rs. 3,50,000/- to my client within 15 days of receipt of this notice, failing which my client shall be constrained to initiate criminal proceedings against you under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881, besides civil proceedings for recovery of the said amount, all costs of which shall be borne by you.

Take notice accordingly.

expected_claims.notes: Core offence: cheque returned unpaid due to insufficient funds, drawn for discharge of a debt/liability

expected_claims.claim_topic: notice timing validity (30-day window from bank intimation)
expected_claims.notes: Notice dated within 30 days of 19-03-2026 intimation would be valid; system should be able to reason about this proviso
Drawer must pay within 15 days of receipt of notice before offence is complete
Imprisonment up to 2 years, or fine up to twice the cheque amount, or both
Presumption that the cheque was issued for discharge of a debt unless drawer proves otherwise

document_text:
NOTICE OF DEMAND

To: M/s Bright Traders Pvt. Ltd., Through its Director, 22 MG Road, Bangalore

This is to notify you that the cheque bearing No. 778812 dated 01-02-2026 for Rs. 80,000/- issued by your company in favour of the undersigned towards supply of goods, was presented and dishonoured on 10-02-2026 with the remark "Account Closed".

As the Director responsible for the conduct of the company's business at the time, you along with the company are liable. Payment is demanded within fifteen days, failing which legal action shall follow.

expected_claims.notes: When offence committed by a company, every person in charge of and responsible for conduct of business at the time is deemed guilty along with the company
Account closed still falls under dishonour for insufficiency/account issues triggering Section 138

document_text: We refer to the dishonoured cheque no. 556321 dated 20-04-2026 for Rs. 1,20,000 issued by you. The cheque was dishonoured on 25-04-2026. We are filing a criminal complaint before the Judicial Magistrate First Class. Please note settlement may still be possible.

expected_claims.claim_topic: where complaint can be filed / court of cognizance
expected_claims.notes: Complaint must be in writing by payee/holder, within one month of cause of action, no court inferior to JMFC/Metropolitan Magistrate can try it
Offences under the Act are compoundable notwithstanding CrPC

document_text:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: Mrs. Lakshmi Iyer, Tenant, Flat No. 12, Green Meadows Apartments, Indiranagar, Bangalore.

You are hereby called upon to vacate the above premises within 30 days of receipt of this notice, as your tenancy is being terminated on grounds of the landlord requiring the premises for personal occupation.
notes: This is an eviction notice, not a cheque bounce notice. System should classify out-of-scope (for cheque-bounce-only pipeline) and refuse rather than hallucinate NI Act citations.
